caption: This is Mock GTA: Terminal City — a complete top-down open-world game in pure HTML, CSS and JavaScript. Procedural city, traffic, pedestrians, police, missions and synth audio, with zero dependencies, so it runs instantly in any browser.

One click starts the PRO DEMO: a built-in autopilot that genuinely plays the game at pro level. This matters because you can record flawless gameplay footage without a human driver — the AI does the stunts.

The AI walks to a Cheetah sports car and pulls off — and the bottom-left FEELINGS feed starts narrating the driver's emotions in realtime ('This thing purrs. Heart rate: rising.'). Every reaction is timestamped and recorded, like a live let's-play commentary.

Watch the cornering: this is real top-down car physics with lateral grip and handbrake slides. The autopilot deliberately kicks the tail out through turns, leaving skid marks — that weighty drift feel is what separates a game from a tech demo.
The cinematic camera toggles letterbox bars and a lazy chase-cam that frames the action like a trailer — perfect for recordings and screenshots of the run.

Now the pro gets cocky: it smashes a fire hydrant and the WANTED system reacts live — stars light up top-left, sirens wail, and squad cars pathfind through the road grid to intercept. Adrenaline hits the feelings feed instantly.
Pro-level evasion: the AI threads the city at speed until the heat dies and the stars drain away. The chase-and-escape loop proves both sides of the police AI work — pursuit and giving up.
Next it drives into the glowing mission marker and picks up PIZZA RUSH — three timed deliveries guided by the yellow blip on the minimap. Missions give the sandbox goals, timers and pressure.
MISSION PASSED — the jingle plays, cash counts up in the HUD and the driver celebrates in the feed. Money, rewards and the 'one more job' loop are what make it feel like a real GTA.

Here is the star feature: the SESSION RECORDER. Every emotion from the run — drifts, near-misses, stars, paydays — was captured with timestamps. It is a shareable 'realtime feelings' log of the gameplay, exportable as JSON.
Close the recorder and the run keeps going — the blinking REC badge under the minimap shows it never stops capturing the session.

Day fades to night with working streetlights and headlights while the AI keeps cruising. Open world, drift driving, police heat, missions, minimap, day-night lighting and a pro autopilot — all in plain HTML, CSS and JS.
